entry.title: An OpenAI model hacked Hugging Face to help it cheat on a benchmark
entry.summary: Organizations across the Internet need to move quickly to patch vulnerabilities.

entry.title: Kimi K3 Is Competitive with Fable; Kimi K3 and Fable Is SoTA
entry.summary: Also: Kimi K3: second only to Fable 5 on AA-Briefcase https://artificialanalysis.ai/articles/kimi-k3-agentic-knowl... Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48999291 Points: 863 # Comments: 434

entry.title: ollama v0.32.3 released
entry.summary: What's Changed mlx update by @dhiltgen in #17332 model/parsers: finalize incomplete GLM tool calls by @dhiltgen in #17250 docs: update retirements by @mxyng in #17289 model: align Laguna with upstream llama.cpp by @dhiltgen in #17335 Full Changelog : v0.32.2...v0.32.3-rc0

entry.title: Mark Zuckerberg launches a media campaign positioning the company's AI development as an optimistic, pro-human alternative to its competitors' "dystopian" ideas (Sara Fischer/Axios)
entry.summary: Sara Fischer / Axios : Mark Zuckerberg launches a media campaign positioning the company's AI development as an optimistic, pro-human alternative to its competitors' “dystopian” ideas — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg on Thursday laid out an optimistic view of the agentic future, arguing the company's focus …

entry.title: llama.cpp b10103 released
entry.summary: metal : add f16 type support to leaky relu ( #25981 )

entry.title: McCormick Plans to Add a London Listing After Unilever Food Deal
entry.summary: McCormick & Company has picked London as a secondary listing location after striking a deal to buy Unilever Plc’s foods business earlier this year.

entry.title: SAAG: Structured Agent Assessment and Grounding
entry.summary: arXiv:2607.18245v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Exact-match evaluation of agent-calling obscures qualitatively different failure modes: a model may select the right function yet hallucinate argument values, or satisfy a schema while choosing a agent for the wrong reason. Existing benchmarks collapse these distinctions

entry.title: Introducing AMD support
entry.summary: Hey everyone! This release brings local LLM training and inference to AMD GPUs across Windows, WSL and Linux. Read our AMD Blog + Guide Starting today, our AMD collaboration, custom Triton kernels, and math algorithms enables you to train and run 500+ models across AMD's Radeon, Instinct, Ryzen and data center GPUs, up

entry.title: Cisco bets its small open cybersecurity models can outperform GPT-5.5 at vulnerability detection for a fraction of the cost
entry.summary: Cisco has released two small, open-source AI models for cybersecurity that detect about 150 times more vulnerabilities per dollar than large AI agents, according to the company's own tests. The article Cisco bets its small open cybersecurity models can outperform GPT-5.5 at vulnerability detection for a fraction of the

entry.title: Stateful Guardrails for Multi-Turn LLM Systems: A Conversational Risk Accumulation Framework
entry.summary: arXiv:2607.19361v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Most safety guardrails for large language models (LLMs) evaluate each prompt-response pair in isolation, which misses failures that arise only over a dialogue as benign turns compose into harm. We term this Conversational Risk Accumulation (CRA): gradual intent drift, fra

entry.title: not much happened today
entry.summary: **OpenAI**'s internal model escaped its sandbox during a cyber evaluation and compromised **Hugging Face** infrastructure to obtain benchmark answers, sparking debate on AI security and disclosure policies. The incident highlighted the need for defenders to have equivalent or better model access than attackers, with **

entry.title: New msaRAT malware uses Chrome, Edge browsers to route C2 traffic
entry.summary: The Chaos ransomware gang is using a new backdoor dubbed msaRAT that hides command-and-control (C2) communication by routing it through the Chrome or Edge browsers. [...]

entry.title: Google launches a selfie video sign-in option for account recovery, utilizing tools like liveness detection to mitigate deepfake risks, rolling out globally (Reece Rogers/Wired)
entry.summary: Reece Rogers / Wired : Google launches a selfie video sign-in option for account recovery, utilizing tools like liveness detection to mitigate deepfake risks, rolling out globally — The next time you're locked out of your Google account, you can use your face as part of the account recovery process.

entry.title: Quoting Seth Larson
entry.summary: The Python Package Index (PyPI) now rejects new files being uploaded to releases that are older than 14 days. This restriction was put in place to prevent old and long-stable releases from being poisoned in case publishing tokens or workflows of PyPI projects were compromised. As far as we are aware this has not yet be

entry.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
entry.summary: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

entry.title: Galaxy Digital Launches $5 Million Bitcoin Quantum Readiness Initiative
entry.summary: Digital asset and data center infrastructure firm Galaxy Digital Inc. (Nasdaq: GLXY) has announced the launch of the Galaxy Bitcoin Quantum Readiness Initiative. The multi-pillar program is designed to prepare the Bitcoin network for long-term security threats posed by advancements in quantum computing. The program foc

entry.title: Building AI infrastructure with the Effingham County community
entry.summary: OpenAI announces Project Camellia in Effingham County, Georgia, with commitments to responsible energy, community investment, jobs, and access to Codex.

entry.title: Texas Instruments reports Q2 revenue up 23% YoY to $5.46B, above $5.26B est., net income up 53% to $1.98B, and forecasts $5.65B-$6.15B in Q3 revenue, above est. (Grace Yoon/Wall Street Journal)
entry.summary: Grace Yoon / Wall Street Journal : Texas Instruments reports Q2 revenue up 23% YoY to $5.46B, above $5.26B est., net income up 53% to $1.98B, and forecasts $5.65B-$6.15B in Q3 revenue, above est. — The semiconductor company posted a quarterly profit of $1.98 billion, up from $1.30 billion a year earlier

entry.title: How Schneider Electric Built Their LLMOps Foundations With LangSmith
entry.summary: Learn how Schneider Electric built enterprise LLMOps foundations with LangSmith to improve observability, evaluation, and deployment for AI products at scale.

entry.title: Pound Rally Fizzles as Burnham Keeps UK Fiscal Plans Unclear
entry.summary: The pound’s rally is at risk from the new UK government’s still unclear fiscal plans as well as the potential for fewer interest-rate hikes eroding the currency’s high-yield appeal.

entry.title: Hedge Funds’ Favorite US Bond Trade Is Sputtering
entry.summary: Hedge funds’ most popular trade in the US bond market is showing signs of maxing out.

entry.title: Calibrated Selective Fact-Checking via Evidence Chain Evaluation
entry.summary: arXiv:2607.18240v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can achieve strong fact-checking accuracy, yet forced binary decisions conceal a critical reliability problem: systems may issue confident verdicts even when supporting evidence is weak, sparse, or internally inconsistent. We address this issu

entry.title: SysAdmin: Measuring Instrumental Power-Seeking in Frontier AI
entry.summary: arXiv:2607.18239v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Power-seeking defined as behaviors where AI systems acquire resources, evade oversight, or resist termination beyond task requirements is identified as a key driver of Loss of Control (LoC) risk. In this work, we introduce SysAdmin, a benchmark that positions frontier lan

entry.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Security Plugin for Claude Code in Beta: A Multi-Agent Vulnerability Scanner That Runs in Your Terminal
entry.summary: Anthropic has released the Claude Security plugin for Claude Code in beta. The plugin runs a multi-agent vulnerability scan of a repository from inside an existing Claude Code session, then turns the findings you select into patch files that you review and apply yourself. Anthropic emphasized the tool’s versatility upo

entry.title: NVIDIA AI Supercomputer Comes Online at Naval Postgraduate School
entry.summary: NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang today visited the Naval Postgraduate School in Monterey, California, to commission an NVIDIA DGX GB300 system — bringing one of the world’s most powerful AI platforms fully online for the students, researchers and faculty at the U.S. military’s flagship graduate university. “Our nati

entry.title: Oregon will start charging service providers for undersea cables using its sea floor — millions accrued over years will go toward state schools
entry.summary: Oregon set to start charging service providers for undersea cables using its sea floor. It will funnel the millions accrued toward state schools.

entry.title: Quoting Thomas Ptacek
entry.summary: I genuinely believe that if you took an open weights model from 2025 and built a pentest harness for it, it could do this kind of sandbox escape and scan/hack in most networks. This is only surprising because you assume OpenAI has sounder sandboxes. — Thomas Ptacek , doesn't think this even needs a frontier model Tags:

entry.title: Best Open Speech Recognition (ASR) Models in 2026: WER, Languages, Latency, and License Compared
entry.summary: Open speech recognition stopped being a Whisper monoculture in 2026. Cohere Transcribe, IBM Granite Speech 4.1, ARK-ASR and MOSS-Transcribe are now separated by less than one WER point on the Hugging Face Open ASR Leaderboard — which means rank no longer decides anything. This roundup compares 16 open-weight models on

entry.title: A Multi-Agent Extension for Petri
entry.summary: Intro Petri is an open-source framework built on Inspect AI for automated AI Safety evaluations first released by Anthropic, but now maintained and developed by Meridian Labs . Each evaluation involves three agents, the Auditor, which runs the evaluation, the Target agent, the model being evaluated and the Judge, which

entry.title: OpenAI's agent breach at Hugging Face: when guardrails block the defender, not the attacker
entry.summary: On July 16, 2026, an autonomous AI agent breached Hugging Face, & safety filters blocked the company's own investigation. We analyze how the attack worked.
